::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Reset your session"}
Experimenting means the occasional wrong turn -- a setting changed, the practice
dataset overwritten, output that suddenly looks different. When something feels
off and you'd like a clean footing without restarting RStudio, run these two
lines in the Console:

```r
## Put jstats' output settings back to their defaults
joutput("standard")   # output level, and decimal places back to 3

## Point saving and loading back at your working directory
joptions(data.dir = "")
```

That resets jstats itself. To clear away objects you created while following
along -- data frames like `MyData_r`, or a value like `MyObject` -- the simplest
way is the little **broom** icon at the top of the **Environment** pane
(upper-right): one click sweeps the pane clean. It clears *everything* in the
Environment, so use it when you're between run-throughs, not in the middle of
one. (You can also remove a single object by name, e.g. `rm(MyData_r)`.) The
built-in practice data comes right back with `jload("community")` whenever you
want it.

This box is the same on every hands-on page in this group.
:::
